title: T-055 â€” Warehouse end-to-end flow tests
prompt:
# T-055 â€” Warehouse end-to-end flow tests

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Add end-to-end tests covering Warehouse P1 critical paths across schema, services, UI, outbox, and prototype parity markers.

## Implementation contract
Cover receive POâ†’LPsâ†’putaway/move/splitâ†’reserve/releaseâ†’FEFO deviationâ†’consume contract stubâ†’expiry cron/overrideâ†’genealogyâ†’label print. Include data-prototype-label selector checks for all first-class labels in prototype-index-warehouse.json.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/e2e/warehouse/** [create]
- packages/db/test/warehouse/** [create]
- _meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-warehouse.json

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the Warehouse E2E suite runs, then GRN from PO creates one LP per operator-entered row and never auto-splits the PO line.
2. Given WH-008 split flow runs, then every output row requires destination and genealogy is asserted.
3. Given labels from prototype-index-warehouse.json are enumerated, then implemented surfaces expose matching data-prototype-label markers or an explicit planned-marker exception for Phase 2 entries.
4. Given all P1 flows complete, then audit/outbox events are asserted for GRN, split/merge, reservation, override, expiry, and label print.

## Test strategy
- RED: write failing Playwright flows with seeded warehouse fixtures.
- GREEN: add DB assertions for LP state, reservations, genealogy, outbox, and audit tables.
- Run pnpm playwright test --grep @warehouse-e2e and pnpm test -- --runInBand warehouse.

## Risk red lines
- Do not skip audit/outbox assertions.
- Do not use mocked authorization for RBAC-sensitive checks.
- Do not treat planned P2 prototype labels as P1 blockers.

cross_module_dependencies.contract: rule_registry, warehouses/locations/printers, feature flags, tenant security policy
PO/TO/WO read models, RM root reservations, WO release/cancel hooks
LP lookup, barcode validation, lock protocol, FEFO suggestion, consume-to-WO
WO material consumption and output LP creation events
